content.summary: CodaMetrix is a Boston-based, privately held SaaS company founded in 2019 that provides AI-powered autonomous medical coding automation to large U.S. health systems. Its flagship CMX CARE™ platform (launched June 2025) uses machine learning, deep learning, and NLP to translate clinical documentation into billing and diagnostic codes across multi-specialty service lines, integrating directly with EHRs (Epic and others). Originally developed at the Massachusetts General Physician Organization (part of Mass General Brigham), the company has grown to serve 220+ hospitals in 27 states, representing ~$180B in net patient revenue — roughly 12% of the U.S. total. CodaMetrix claims average outcomes of >60% reduction in coding costs, 70% reduction in manual coding, and a 5-week acceleration in cash collections. It was rated #1 Best in KLAS for Autonomous Coding in 2026. The company is Series B-funded ($40M raised in March 2024, led by Transformation Capital, with SignalFire and Frist Cressey Ventures participating). A Series A was led by SignalFire with strategic co-investors including Mass General Brigham, Yale Medicine, and Frist Cressey Ventures. Headcount is estimated at 101–200 employees. Key buyers are health system CFOs, Chief Revenue Officers, and VP-level revenue cycle and HIM leaders. Strong buying signals include the 2025 platform relaunch, active leadership hiring, an AI Quality Council with marquee health systems, and strong KLAS recognition.
content.industry: AI-powered Healthcare Revenue Cycle Management / Medical Coding Automation

Natural Language Processing (NLP) — clinical documentation parsing
Amazon Web Services (AWS) — cloud infrastructure (SOC 2 certified on AWS)

content.total_funding: $40M+ (Series B alone was $40M, closed March/April 2024; Series A amount not publicly disclosed; total across both rounds not confirmed)
buying_signals: Rated #1 Best in KLAS for Autonomous Medical Coding (2026) — top industry recognition driving inbound pipeline
New product platform launch: CMX CARE™ (Contextual Coding Automation) and Emergency Department module introduced June 2025 — active technology expansion and new use-case penetration
Rapid market footprint growth: customers now represent $180B in Net Patient Revenue (~12% of U.S. total), 220 hospitals across 27 states — significant scale milestone reached in 2025
Leadership expansion (2025): hired Chief Growth Officer (Gail Airasian), restructured executive team — signals transition into high-growth commercial phase
Closed $40M Series B in March/April 2024 — fresh growth capital deployed for sales, engineering, and market expansion
Actively hiring pathology coding analysts and remote/hybrid roles (mid-2026) — workforce scaling in progress
Launched first external AI hackathon 'CodaMetrix Calling' (June 2026) — signals investment in AI talent and ecosystem development
Published coding-quality whitepaper and buyer's guide (June 2026) — active demand-generation targeting revenue cycle leadership buyers

Launched AI Coding Quality Council with Mayo Clinic, Henry Ford Health, OHSU, Allegheny Health Network, UC Medicine (2026) — ecosystem orchestration and thought leadership expansion indicating enterprise deal activity
Customer co-investment: health system clients representing $40B NPR became investors — unusually strong signal of customer confidence and lock-in
content.headcount_estimate: 101–200 employees (LinkedIn shows 112 listed employees; Crunchbase range: 101–250; as of July 2026)
